data.title: Sable shares jump after federal approval of California pipeline
data.description:
A U.S. pipeline regulator has cleared the way for Sable Offshore Corp. (SOC) to restart a controversial oil pipeline along California’s coast, a decade after a spill released thousands of barrels of crude near Santa Barbara.

Sable said the Department of Transportation’s Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration (PHMSA) approved its restart plan for portions of the Las Flores Pipeline System. Shares of the Houston-based company jumped about 30% on Tuesday following the announcement.

The decision follows PHMSA’s determination last week that key segments of the pipeline fall under federal, rather than California, jurisdiction, a finding that is likely to be challenged by state officials and environmental groups.

Sable has been seeking to revive offshore oil production assets it acquired from Exxon Mobil (XOM) last year, but progress has been slowed by opposition in California, where memories of the 2015 Refugio spill remain fresh. That accident, tied to a pipeline then known as Line 901, fouled beaches, damaged tourism and killed wildlife.

PHMSA approved the restart of two lines, CA-324 and CA-325, which move crude oil from coastal pump stations inland. CA-324 was the line responsible for the Refugio spill when it was operated by Plains All American.

California lawmakers and environmental advocates criticized the federal action, arguing it sidesteps state oversight and increases the risk of another spill. State officials said they are reviewing PHMSA’s jurisdictional determination and weighing potential responses.

Environmental groups, including the Center for Biological Diversity, said they are prepared to pursue legal options, noting that a federal consent decree tied to the 2015 spill remains in effect and still requires state approvals.

The dispute underscores broader tensions between federal efforts to expand domestic oil production under President Donald Trump and California’s push to restrict drilling and protect coastal ecosystems.

data.title: What investors can expect from Costco in 2026
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) enters 2026 with solid operating momentum but a sluggish share price performance over the last year amid concerns about valuation. While the stock has continued to outperform broader retail averages over the past five years, debate persists about when the next rally might begin.

 

 

 

 

Operationally, Costco (COST) is exiting 2025 with high-single-digit top-line growth and mid-single-digit comparable sales, a pace that looks sustainable into 2026 if macro conditions hold. With its first-quarter earnings report, Costco (COST) reported total sales increased 8.2% to $66.0 billion and comparable sales rose 6.4%. At the end of the quarter, Costco (COST) operated 923 warehouses, including 633 in the United States and Puerto Rico, 114 in Canada, 42 in Mexico, 37 in Japan, 29 in the United Kingdom, 20 in Korea, and 15 in Australia. Costco (COST) is planning around 35 new warehouses in fiscal 2026, including relocations.

Membership fee income rose faster than total sales for Costco (COST) during its last fiscal year and continues to be a core earnings driver. Another bright spot is e-commerce, where Costco (COST) has outpaced Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), and Amazon (AMZN). Costco (COST) has increased its tech prowess in general, including rolling out pre-scanning of baskets before checkout and mobile Scan & Go / Scan & Pay programs where members scan items with their phones, pay in-app, and show a QR code at exit. On the AI front, Costco (COST) is using advanced AI to sharpen inventory flows, model demand trends, run personalized marketing campaigns, and improve product search effectiveness.

For investors, Costco's (COST) strong balance sheet and free cash flow profile keep the door open to another sizable special dividend in 2026, though timing is uncertain since it has only been since early 2024 when the last special dividend was announced. After a powerful multi-year run, Costco (COST) is a logical candidate for a stock split if management wants to keep the nominal share price more accessible, especially after peers like Walmart (WMT) and other retail leaders have split in the last few years.

Wall Street analysts have a consensus Buy rating on Costco (COST), while Seeking Alpha analysts are more cautious with a consensus Hold rating.

data.title: Trump vents over 'good news is bad news' dynamics
data.description:
President Donald Trump reminisced about "the old days," noting that "when there was good news, the Market went up," in a social media post on Tuesday.  "Nowadays, when there is good news, the Market goes down, because everybody thinks that Interest Rates will be immediately lifted to take care of 'potential' Inflation."

He lauded the Q3 GDP report, saying it grew 4.2%, beating the 2.5% rate that was predicted. According to the Bureau of Economic Analysis, Q3 GDP rose at an annualized rate of 4.3%. That compared with the consensus of +3.2%.

Trump connected markets dynamics with his pick for the next Fed Chair. "I want my new Fed Chairman to lower Interest Rates if the Market is doing well, not destroy the Market for no reason whatsoever," he wrote on Truth Social. "I want to have a Market the likes of which we haven’t had in many decades, a Market that goes up on good news, and down on bad news, the way it should be, and the way it was."

As for inflation, that "will take care of itself and, if it doesn’t, we can always raise Rates at the appropriate time," he said. "But the appropriate time is not to kill Rallies, which could lift our Nation by 10, 15, and even 20 GDP points in a year — and maybe even more than that!"

The GDP report, issued on Tuesday, indicated that inflation remained above the Fed's 2% target. The PCE price index rose 2.8% in Q3 vs. +2.1% in Q2, and core PCE, which excludes food and energy, climbed 2.8% vs. +2.1% in the prior quarter.

Currently, traders expect the Fed to keep rates unchanged at 3.50%-3.75% at its January 27-28 meeting, with an 86.7% probability. For the March meeting, trading indicates a 54.4% chance that the rate will remain unchanged, a 40.7% probability it will be cut by 25 basis points to 3.25%-3.50%, and a 5.0% probability the benchmark rate will drop to 3.00%-3.25%, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Here are the most and least likely M&A targets in biotech, according to Truist
data.description:
French biotech (ABVX) has emerged as the most likely acquisition target among biotechs in 2026, significantly outpacing others, while liver drug developer Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL) has become the least likely candidate, according to a survey conducted by Truist Securities.

The company has received 22 investor mentions (44% of surveyed investors) in its proprietary survey commissioned to gauge market sentiment, Truist analysts led by Gregory Renza wrote in a research note last week.

Shares of the Paris-based biotech have skyrocketed this year, with multiple reports indicating that Eli Lilly (LLY) is pursuing an M&A deal with the company whose lead asset, obefazimod, is targeted at inflammatory conditions such as ulcerative colitis.

The brokerage retained its Buy rating and $140 per share target on Abivax (ABVX), arguing that with a YTD rally of 1,600% and a positive late-stage readout for obefazimod in July, “the stock has maintained high investor interest into 2026.”

Meanwhile, Revolution Medicines (RVMD) has gotten the attention of only 8% of surveyed investors, falling far short of Abivax (ABVX), while CG Oncology (CGON) and Centessa Pharmaceuticals (CNTA) scored 6% each.

Among the least likely buyout targets, Madrigal (MDGL), which markets MASH therapy Rezdiffra, has topped the list with 14 mentions, followed by Janux Therapeutics (JANX), Solid Biosciences (SLDB), and Arcus Biosciences (RCUS), indicating investor uncertainty about their M&A prospects. 

“The data show a clear concentration of acquisition expectations around ABVX, signaling strong market speculation or perceived strategic fit,” Truist wrote.

data.title: Ares Management open to buying large private equity group, CEO says
data.description:
Ares Management (ARES) CEO Michael Arougheti said Tuesday that his alternative asset management firm is open to acquiring a big private equity group to strengthen its leveraged buyout unit, according to a media report on Tuesday.

A potential acquisition comes as U.S. retirement plans start exploring how to implement private assets, he told the Financial Times in an interview, giving Ares (ARES) the scale to compete with larger PE rivals firms Blackstone (BX), KKR (KKR) and Apollo Global Management (APO).

“I could see a world where we look to expand the private equity franchise, either by getting larger, geographically diversifying, looking at sector-specific capabilities that would be additive to other parts of the franchise,” Arougheti said. “In this world of defined contribution plans opening up to alternatives over time, diversified private equity will become more and more important,” he added.

He told the FT that Ares (ARES) has "a lot of financial capacity to buy," while noting no deal was imminent. Private equity represents 4.2% of the company's current assets, vs. 13.5% in 2014, when Ares went public.

Last year, Ares (ARES) explored a potential tie-up with French PE group Astorg, but ultimately, the talks fell through, the FT reported, citing four people briefed on the matter.

data.title: Robinhood is the top performing investment banking stock YTD
data.description:
The S&P 500 financials sector has largely kept pace with the broader market in 2025, rising about +14% year to date.

Financials rank as the fourth-best performing sector in the S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the broader financial sector, below is a list of the top 10 investment banking and brokerage stocks, ranked by their YTD performance. The list includes SA Quant Rating.

Robinhood Markets (HOOD) leads the list with an impressive YTD performance of +228.42%. Futu Holdings (FUTU) and Goldman Sachs (GS) follow in second and third place, with YTD performances of +106.89% and +57%, respectively. Interactive Brokers Group (IBKR) and StoneX Group (SNEX) round out the top five.

Notably, Futu Holdings (FUTU), Interactive Brokers Group (IBKR), and Nomura (NMR) all carry a “Strong Buy” Quant Rating. Morgan Stanley (MS), Charles Schwab (SCHW), XP (XP), and Evercore (EVR) complete the rest of the top ten performers.

Here is the list:

  1. Robinhood Markets (HOOD), YTD perf: +228.42%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.47)

  2. Futu Holdings (FUTU), YTD perf: +106.89%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.76)

  3. Goldman Sachs (GS), YTD perf: +57.00%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.45)

  4. Interactive Brokers (IBKR), YTD perf: +48.97%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.64)

  5. StoneX Group (SNEX), YTD perf: +47.93%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.40)

  6. Nomura Holdings (NMR), YTD perf: +43.35%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.66)

  7. Morgan Stanley (MS), YTD perf: +42.98%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.44)

  8. Charles Schwab (SCHW), YTD perf: +37.02%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.41)

  9. XP (XP), YTD perf: +36.79%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.17)

  10. Evercore (EVR), YTD perf: +26.71%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.17)

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO)

data.title: Levi Strauss's growth levers insulates business from import tariffs -- Raymond James
data.description:
An iconic name with growth levers across channels, geographies, customers, and categories, Levi Strauss & Co (LEVI) has the potential to see earnings grow by at least high-single-digits and revenue increasing by mid-single-digits (percentages).

Accordingly, Raymond James initiates coverage of the 172-year-old blue jeans business with an Outperform rating and $26 price target, implying 23% upside to the stock’s closing price on Monday.

Levi’s (LEVI) growth levers will enable the company to achieve analyst Rick Patel’s targets, including: Direct to consumer strength, an attractive international runway, especially in Asia; and, strong momentum in the women’s segment.

And although Levi Strauss (LEVI) faces similar tariff-related challenges as its peers (imports from Bangladesh, Cambodia, Vietnam, Turkey, Pakistan, and Mexico with only its premium brands made in the U.S.), Patel believes Levi’s (LEVI) has enough “gross margin tailwinds for expansion that will more than offset tariffs in FY26.”

The company has outperformed the S&P 500 this year with a YTD gain of 23%, as well as peers like Kontoor Brands (KTB), Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF), and PVH Corp (PVH), all three of which are in the red this year.

data.title: Bitcoin has never surpassed $100K in inflation-adjusted terms, Alex Thorn says
data.description:
Although bitcoin (BTC-USD) peaked above $126K in October, that record falls below $100K once inflation is taken into account, Alex Thorn, Galaxy Digital's (GLXY) global research head, said on Tuesday.

Measured in 2020 dollars, bitcoin (BTC-USD) actually peaked this year at $99,848, Thorn, who last month lowered his BTC price target, wrote in an X post. 

He explained that the inflation-adjusted measure "adjusts for [Consumer Price Index] decline in purchasing power incrementally across every inflation print 2020 to present."

In nominal terms, BTC changed hands at $87.7K in afternoon trading, down about 1% in the past 24 hours and 7% year-to-date.

data.title: Market Voices: Paramount-WBD, Trump vs. defense sector, Wegovy pill
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks.

  • Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) investor Harris Oakmark said that Paramount Skydance's (PSKY) amended bid for the company is still insufficient.

"The changes in Paramount’s new offer were necessary, but not sufficient," said Alex Fitch, Harris Oakmark's director of U.S. Research, in an email to Reuters. "We see the two deals as a toss-up, and there is a cost to changing paths. If Paramount is serious about winning, they’re going to need to provide a greater incentive."

Harris Oakmark held 96 million WBD shares, equivalent to a roughly 4% stake, at the end of September, making it WBD's fifth-largest shareholder, Reuters added.

  • President Trump said he plans to urge defense contractors to spend more money on increasing production and less on stock buybacks and executive compensation. 

“They want to buy back their stock—I don’t want them to buy back their stock,” Trump told reporters late Monday, according to The Wall Street Journal. “I want them to put the money in plants and equipment.”

Trump also cited Lockheed Martin (LMT) as a contractor that needed to upgrade its manufacturing facilities to speed up production. 

“We have many people [who] want the F-35 fighter jet, and it takes too long to deliver them to allies or to ourselves,” Trump said. “The only way they’re going to be able to deliver them is if they build new plants. They don’t want to build new plants because it’s expensive.”

Trump is expected to meet with executives of the nation's top defense contractors next week in Florida. 

The Journal added that there has been speculation that Trump may issue an executive order banning defense contractors from engaging in stock buybacks. 

  • Novo Nordisk (NVO) announced it has received FDA approval for a pill formulation of its hugely popular GLP-1 weight-loss drug Wegovy, which up until now was typically administered by injection.

“As the first oral GLP-1 treatment for people living with overweight or obesity, the Wegovy pill provides patients with a new, convenient treatment option that can help patients start or continue their weight loss journey,” said Novo Nordisk CEO Mike Doustdar in a statement released late Monday.

"We are confident that the expansion of Wegovy to a pill will help patients who may have not sought or accepted treatment before," said Dave Moore, Novo Nordisk's EVP of US Operations, in a second statement.

Wegovy's main competitor in the weight-loss market has been Eli Lilly's (LLY) Zepbound, which is also delivered via injection. Novo Nordisk also markets Wegovy for diabetes under the name Ozempic, while Lilly markets Zepbound for diabetes under the name Mounjaro.

data.title: European market rebound led by pharma gains; STOXX 600 logs new record high
data.description:
Update: The post reflects European markets closing indices.

London (UKX) +0.12% at 9,877. 

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.10% at 24,307. 

France (CAC:IND) -0.08% at 8,114. 

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.34% higher to 588.7, to reach a fresh record high. The pharmaceutical sector advances led to broader market strength. Novo Nordisk (NVO) surged nearly 10% after the company received approval from the US FDA to market an oral version of its obesity treatment.

Banks rose alongside strong Eurozone bond performance.

On the geopolitical front, Russia and Ukraine continued to intensify attacks on each other, a day after the Kremlin said weekend peace talks failed to produce a breakthrough.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was unchanged at 4.17%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 4.51%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was unchanged at 2.87%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article
